title: Level 333
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 333: '''"Noctilucence"'''}}
{{Quote|The attributes of liminality are necessarily ambiguous... Liminal entities are neither here nor there; they are betwixt and between the positions assigned and arrayed by law, custom, convention and ceremonial.|Victor Turner}}
{{Class Variable}}
{{Class Variable}}
'''Level 333''', or '''Noctilucence''', is a complex level consisting of three distinct areas, being the retail, parking, and rooftop zones. ---- {{Blurimage|file=Noctilucence1.jpg|hovertext=Consumerism|caption=An image of The Retail Zone.}} ==='''The Retail Zone'''=== Serving as the entrance point of '''Level 333''', '''The Retail Zone''' is a vast, empty supermarket resembling a typical big-box store from the early 70s, with robust walls of gray concrete and a shiny linoleum floor. Posters adorn the walls at regular intervals, advertising seemingly nonexistent products. Many of the hanging fluorescent lights scattered throughout the level are nonfunctional, leading to it being noticeably poorly lit, with wanderers reporting a strange feeling of discomfort emanating from its dark corners. The only sounds that can be heard are the buzzing of the fluorescent lights overhead and the occasional unintelligible announcements that can be heard all across Level 333. One of the main characteristics of '''The Retail Zone''' is its cryptic announcements. They often come across as nonsensical gibberish, as if they're in another, unintelligible language. While they may consist of English words, their pronunciation is often unnatural. If one happens to hear these cryptic messages, it is imperative that they seek shelter immediately. These transmissions are not to be trusted, as, in reality, they are the calls of entities that hope to attract unsuspecting travelers. Those who fail to recognize the danger in time may find themselves in a very precarious situation.
{{Quote|Those announcements... I don't know what was up with them. The voice was soulless, and the words were... complete gibberish.|J.C. Collin}}
Another peculiar feature of this level is its ability to personalize the products that appear for each wanderer. On the usually desolate aisles of the supermarket, one may find their favorite drink, a CD they enjoy listening to or, most disturbing, a photo with their loved ones. Even though these items might seem harmless at first, it is strongly advised that they are not to be touched. Within the store, one may find many objects and consumables, both useful and useless. For example, one might find fresh water in one of the many fridges, or rotten vegetables in the produce aisle that emit a foul odor. This section is often considered as the least safe, as numerous entities, disguised as unsuspecting employees roam the halls of the super-store. Most of the time, they will be wearing a red uniform. Among the most dangerous are '''[[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]]''' and '''[[Entity 3|Smilers]].''' It has also been confirmed that these violent beings are responsible for creating and transmitting the nonsensical announcements that echo throughout the level. These beings frequently try to mimic the behavior of one's close friends and family, hoping that they let their guard down so they can strike. To this day, no outposts have been spotted. === '''The Parking Zone''' === {{Blurimage|file=Noctiparking.jpg|hovertext=Subterranean Isolation|caption=An image of The Parking Zone.}} '''The Parking Zone''' is the second of the three areas that can be found within the premises of '''Level 333'''. J.C. Collin describes this area as being ''"Extremely dangerous, unsettling and unhygienic."'' This part of the level is extremely empty. Despite this being a parking garage, no cars can be found on the premises of the level. In fact, the only objects that appear are random empty shopping carts, scattered around the Garage. The temperature in this zone is relatively high, averaging around 28°C. The air is humid, and mold is very common. This area is extremely hard to reach, as the only entry points are certain flights of stairs that appear randomly within the Retail Zone. Upon entering this level, a foul odor fills the air, and the atmosphere is surreal and disorienting. The ambiance is unsettling, contributing to the already high sense of unease that one feels in this strange level. Each parking spot is marked with an unrecognizable combination of letters and numbers, belonging to a strange and indecipherable alphabet, further deepening the mystery of the place. Many entities roam this dark and mysterious area, including '''[[Entity 36|Lighters]]''', '''[[Entity 3|Smilers]]''', '''[[Entity 35|Spines]]''', and '''[[Entity 38|Needlelimbs]]'''. It is strongly advised that upon encountering any of these creatures, one should immediately flee the area to avoid being caught. In addition to the lurking threats, strange announcements can still be heard echoing throughout the expanse of this parking garage. Unlike those in the Retail Zone, however, these announcements are intelligible, using proper English and grammar. Despite their clarity, these messages should not be trusted. Whatever one does, they are explicitly warned not to follow the commands issued by these communiqués, as they are often part of a deceptive trap designed to lure wanderers into further danger. No outposts have been spotted to this day. ==='''The Rooftop Zone'''=== {{Blurimage|file=Rooftop333.jpg|hovertext=Finality|caption=An image of The Rooftop Zone.}} '''The Rooftop Zone''' is the third and final area of Level 333. It is almost universally considered the safest and most secure of the three zones. The temperature is notably colder compared to the other two levels, remaining a constant 8°C, the air being particularly frigid. Strangely, there is no visible sky above, adding to the eerie atmosphere of the area. Amid the otherwise desolate expanse, a single car can be found, placed randomly within the zone. Wanderers are able to enter the car and even take supplies left inside, such as bottles of [[Object 1|'''almond water''']]. However, despite these seemingly helpful provisions, the car cannot be started. Not much else is known about this area, as very few people have ventured into it to this day. No entities or outposts have been spotted here, and the zone seems to function as a rare safe haven for those in need of rest or a temporary escape from the dangers of the other areas. {{Underline|FFFFFF}} =='''An interview with J.C. Collin'''== J.C. Collin is the only survivor of Level 333. This interview takes place on March 3rd, 2001.
'''Interviewer''': Hello, J.C. Collin. Today, I will be asking you some questions referring to your experience on Level 333. '''JC:''' Alright. '''Interviewer''': Firstly, if you could describe the level with one word, what would it be? '''JC''': Well, I think the most fitting word would be "Deceptive". Many entities try to deceive you, mimicking the body language of your loved ones. The announcements "hypnotise" you, trying to make you follow their commands and the items that you find throughout the level are dangerous. '''Interviewer''': Alright. How much time did you spend in this level? '''JC''': Not much. I figured that the only way to escape was to try and jump off the roof, as the building wasn't very tall. I don't remember how I got to the rooftop, though... '''Interviewer''': Did you encounter any entities? '''JC''': Surprisingly, no. The place was devoid of any life-forms. '''Interviewer''': How did you enter Level 333? '''JC''': From what I remember, I encountered a very bright source of light in Level 638, I believe. After approaching it, I was suddenly teleported here. Perhaps that’s the reason Level 333 is named Noctilucence in the first place! '''Interviewer''': Alright, JC. Thank you for your time.
{{Underline|FFFFFF}} =='''Entrances and Exits'''== To this day, only a handful of methods for entering and exiting Level 333 have been reliably reported. These passages remain elusive and unpredictable. The exact nature of these entry and exit points continues to be a mystery, further contributing to the enigma surrounding this level. === '''Entrances''' === # Finding a door with a number "333" on [[Level 332]]'''.'''
# === '''Exits''' === # Jumping off The Rooftop into the void will send one to a random level in The Backrooms. # Getting into the car on The Rooftop has a chance of sending one to [[The Hub|'''The Hub''']] or [https://backrooms-freewriting.fandom.com/wiki/Level_69 Level 69]. # A metal door that is located on the wall of Level 333 has a chance of leading one to [[Level 49]]. The door is locked and requires a code to open. The code is displayed on one of the monitors in The Control Room. # The door at the right of the locked metal door to Level 49 is made out of wood with a few scattered painting frames and paintings. Opening the door whose made of wood can open to an room with a big painting on the front wall of the room. Touching it will noclip you through the painting–a painting in [https://backrooms-freewriting.fandom.com/wiki/Level_57 Level 57]. # Touching a unnaturally black wall sends you to [[Level 6]]. {{Underline|FFFFFF}}
